meta.title: PV Collection
meta.description: A personal coin and currency collection spanning the world

items.title: 10 Rupees — Black Boat (Fafda Note)
items.description: Vintage Indian 10 rupee banknote featuring a dhow (sailing boat) on the reverse. Known colloquially as the 'Fafda Note' for its distinctive dark colour. Issued by the Reserve Bank of India during the post-independence era.

items.title: Solmon Island 5 Dollar
items.description: The Solomon Islands is a sovereign archipelagic nation in Melanesia, South Pacific, consisting of over 900 islands east of Papua New Guinea. Its capital, Honiara, is on Guadalcanal Island. Known for its WWII, history, diving, and rich culture, the nation features volcanic, forested terrain. The economy relies on agriculture, fishing, and forestry, with English as the official language

items.title: Cook Island Commerative
items.description: The Cook Islands are a self-governing nation in the South Pacific, in free association with New Zealand, comprised of 15 islands known for turquoise lagoons, beaches, and volcanic peaks

items.title: Costa Rica 2MIL
items.description: 2 MIL Colones

items.title: North Ireland 10Pound P-W344
items.description: Vertical Note, Issue by Ulster Bank

items.title: South Carolina State Fantasy Commerative Note
items.description: This note is not official government currency; it is a commemorative polymer souvenir note produced by a private company called Applied Currency Concepts.

items.title: British Armed Force 10 New Pence
items.description: British Armed Forces currency consisted of special vouchers and banknotes issued between 1946 and 1972 (1st–6th series) for use in NAAFI canteens and by personnel in occupied zones to prevent black market activity. These notes ranged from 3 pence up to 5 pounds, with later series in 1972 introducing decimal 'New Pence'

items.title: Aldabra Island
items.description: Aldabra Island, part of the Seychelles, uses the Seychellois Rupee (SCR) as its official currency. As a remote, uninhabited UNESCO World Heritage site managed by the Seychelles Islands Foundation, there are no banks, so visitors—usually on specialized expedition cruises—should carry Euros or USD. Note that "Aldabra Islands" currency notes often listed online are collector/novelty items.

items.title: Antarctica 1 Dollar
items.description: Antarctic dollar, or Antarctician dollar, that's used throughout the Federated States of Antarctica. It's also known as an Emp (or buck) in honor of the Emperor Penguins that call Antarctica home

items.title: Scoland 5 Pound
items.description: Scotland is a country that is part of the United Kingdom. It contains nearly one-third of the United Kingdom's land area, consisting of the northern part of the island of Great Britain and more than 790 adjacent islands, principally in the archipelagos of the Hebrides and the Northern Isles. The official currency of Scotland is the Pound Sterling (£/GBP), which is the same currency used throughout the rest of the United Kingdom

items.title: Tatarstan 100 Ruble
items.description: Tatarstan, officially the Republic of Tatarstan, sometimes also called Tataria, is a republic of Russia located in Eastern Europe. It is a part of the Volga Federal District; and its capital and largest city is Kazan, an important cultural centre in Russia. Tatarstan is a republic within the Russian Federation and uses the Russian ruble (RUB) as its official legal tender. While the republic issued temporary "currency checks" or social coupons between 1991 and 1994 to manage shortages, these are no longer in use, and all transactions are conducted in rubles.

items.title: 10 Hong Kong Dollar
items.description: The Hong Kong dollar is the official currency of Hong Kong. It is divided into 100 cents. Historically, it was also divided into 1000 mils. The Hong Kong Monetary Authority is the monetary authority of Hong Kong and the Hong Kong dollar.

items.title: 1 Hong Kong Cent
items.description: The Hong Kong dollar is the official currency of Hong Kong. It is divided into 100 cents. Historically, it was also divided into 1000 mils. The Hong Kong Monetary Authority is the monetary authority of Hong Kong and the Hong Kong dollar.

items.title: 50 Hong Kong Dollar
items.description: The Hong Kong dollar is the official currency of Hong Kong. It is divided into 100 cents. Historically, it was also divided into 1000 mils. The Hong Kong Monetary Authority is the monetary authority of Hong Kong and the Hong Kong dollar.

items.title: Yugoslavia 5 Billion Dinara
items.description: Yugoslavia was a Balkan country (1918–1992) created as a union of South Slavic peoples, evolving from a monarchy into a socialist federal republic under Josip Broz Tito. Comprising six republics—Bosnia-Herzegovina, Croatia, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, and Slovenia—it was known for its independent, non-aligned stance during the Cold War. Following Tito's death in 1980, rising nationalism and economic crisis led to its violent dissolution

items.title: Yugoslavia 50 Dinara
items.description: Yugoslavia was a Balkan country (1918–1992) created as a union of South Slavic peoples, evolving from a monarchy into a socialist federal republic under Josip Broz Tito. Comprising six republics—Bosnia-Herzegovina, Croatia, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, and Slovenia—it was known for its independent, non-aligned stance during the Cold War. Following Tito's death in 1980, rising nationalism and economic crisis led to its violent dissolution

items.title: Biafra 1 Pound
items.description: The Republic of Biafra was a short-lived secessionist state in southeastern Nigeria that existed from May 30, 1967, to January 1970. Led by Lieutenant Colonel Odumegwu Ojukwu, it declared independence due to ethnic tensions, violence against Igbos, and political disagreements, triggering the brutal Nigerian Civil War. Following a strict blockade, the war caused severe famine, killing an estimated 500,000 to 3 million people

items.title: Transnistria 1 Ruble
items.description: Transnistria is an unrecognized, self-proclaimed breakaway state located between the Dniester River and the Ukrainian border, internationally recognized as part of Moldova. It functions as a de facto independent entity with its own government, military, currency, and police force, heavily supported by Russia, which maintains peacekeepers in the region

items.title: New Zealand 5 Dollar
items.description: New Zealand is an island country in the southwestern Pacific Ocean. It consists of two main landmasses—the North Island and the South Island —and over 600 smaller islands

items.title: Vanuatu 200 Vatu
items.description: Vanuatu, officially the Republic of Vanuatu, is an island country in Melanesia located in the South Pacific Ocean. The archipelago, which is of volcanic origin, is 1,750 km east of northern Australia, 540 km northeast of New Caledonia, east of New Guinea, southeast of Solomon Islands, and west of Fiji

items.title: PNG 2 Kina
items.description: Papua New Guinea (PNG) is a culturally and biologically diverse nation in Oceania, north of Australia, occupying the eastern half of New Guinea Island. With over 800 languages, 11.78 million people, and a capital in Port Moresby, it is a rugged land of mountains, volcanoes, and rainforests that achieved independence from Australia in 1975

items.title: Cayman Island 1  Dollar
items.description: The Cayman Islands is a premier British Overseas Territory in the western Caribbean, comprising Grand Cayman, Cayman Brac, and Little Cayman. Known for its high-end tourism, tax-neutral status, and major financial sector, the islands feature top-tier attractions like Seven Mile Beach, Stingray City, and world-class diving

items.title: State of Jersey 1 Pound
items.description: New Jersey is a Mid-Atlantic U.S. state known as the "Garden State," bordered by New York, the Atlantic Ocean, Delaware, and Pennsylvania. As the most densely populated state and 11th-most populous, it features the capital city of Trenton and major city Newark. It was the third state to ratify the U.S. Constitution

items.title: Australia
items.description: Australia, officially the Commonwealth of Australia, is a country comprising the mainland of the Australian continent, the island of Tasmania and numerous smaller islands. It has a land area of 7,688,287 km², making it the sixth-largest country in the world

items.title: Tonga
items.description: Tonga, officially the Kingdom of Tonga, is a Polynesian archipelago nation in the South Pacific with a population of approximately 104,000–109,000. Its capital is Nuku'alofa, located on the main island of Tongatapu, which houses roughly 70% of the population. The culture is deeply Christian and rooted in traditional values of respect, community, and humility, with a constitutional monarchy over 1,000 years old. The currency is the Tongan paʻanga (TOP)

items.title: Abkhazia 10 Apsar
items.description: Abkhazia, officially the Republic of Abkhazia, is a partially recognised state in the South Caucasus. It sits on the eastern coast of the Black Sea, at the intersection of Eastern Europe and West Asia. It covers 8,665 square kilometres and has a population of around 245,000. Its capital and largest city is Sukhumi

items.title: Transnistria Plastic Coin Set
items.description: Transnistria is an unrecognized, self-proclaimed breakaway state located between the Dniester River and the Ukrainian border, internationally recognized as part of Moldova. It functions as a de facto independent entity with its own government, military, currency, and police force, heavily supported by Russia, which maintains peacekeepers in the region

items.title: Iceland Kronr
items.description: Iceland, a Nordic island nation, is defined by its dramatic landscape with volcanoes, geysers, hot springs and lava fields. Massive glaciers are protected in Vatnajökull and Snæfellsjökull national parks. Most of the population lives in the capital, Reykjavik, which runs on geothermal power and is home to the National and Saga museums, tracing Iceland’s Viking history.

items.title: Fiji 5 Dollar 2025
items.description: Fiji, a country in the South Pacific, is an archipelago of more than 300 islands. It's famed for rugged landscapes, palm-lined beaches and coral reefs with clear lagoons. Its major islands, Viti Levu and Vanua Levu, contain most of the population. Viti Levu is home to the capital, Suva, a port city with British colonial architecture

items.title: Samoa 5 Tala
items.description: Samoa, officially the Independent State of Samoa, is an island country in Polynesia, part of Oceania, in the South Pacific Ocean. It consists of two main islands, two smaller, inhabited islands, and several smaller, uninhabited islands, including the Aleipata Islands.

items.title: NETHERLANDS INDIES (Imperial Japanese Government)
items.description: The Netherlands Indies (or Dutch East Indies) was a Dutch colony consisting of present-day Indonesia, established in 1800 following the nationalization of the Dutch East India Company (VOC) and lasting until 1949. The capital was Batavia (now Jakarta). It was known for intense spice trade, significant agricultural exploitation, and Japanese occupation during World War II.

items.title: Japanese occupation of Burma 10 Rupees
items.description: The Japanese occupation of Burma was the period between 1942 and 1945 during World War II, when Burma was occupied by the Empire of Japan. The Japanese had assisted formation of the Burma Independence Army, and trained the Thirty Comrades, who were the founders of the modern Armed Forces.

items.title: Aruba 10 Florin
items.description: Aruba, officially the Country of Aruba, is a constituent island country within the Kingdom of the Netherlands, in the southern Caribbean Sea 29 kilometres north of the Venezuelan peninsula of Paraguaná and 80 kilometres northwest of Curaçao

items.title: Gibraltar 10 Shillings
items.description: Gibraltar is a British Overseas Territory located at the southern tip of the Iberian Peninsula, guarding the entrance to the Mediterranean Sea. Spanning \(6.8 \text{ km}^2\), it is famous for the 426m-high Rock of Gibraltar, a dense town, and its wild Barbary Macaques. It is a strategic, self-governing territory with a, economy driven by tourism and finance.

items.title: Czechoslovakia 10 Korun
items.description: Czechoslovakia was a Central European country formed in 1918 from the Austro-Hungarian Empire, comprising modern-day Czech Republic and Slovakia. It existed as a democratic republic (1918–1938), was occupied by Nazi Germany during WWII, and was later under Soviet communist rule (1948–1989). The nation split peacefully into the Czech Republic and Slovakia on January 1, 1993, a separation known as the "Velvet Divorce"

items.title: USSR - Soviet Union 5 Rubles
items.description: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR or Soviet Union) was a transcontinental socialist state in Eurasia that existed from 1922 to 1991, functioning as the world's first communist nation. It was a union of 15 republics led by the Communist Party, acting as a major global superpower and the primary rival to the U.S. during the Cold War.

items.title: Pitcairn Island Fantasy 20Dollar
items.description: Pitcairn Island is a remote British Overseas Territory in the South Pacific, famous as the refuge for the 1790 Bounty mutineers and their Tahitian companions. With a population of under 50, it is the only inhabited island in a four-island group, relying on subsistence farming, fishing, and tourism. The capital is Adamstown.

items.title: Albania 200 Leke
items.description: Albania, on Southeastern Europe’s Balkan Peninsula, is a small country with Adriatic and Ionian coastlines and an interior crossed by the Albanian Alps. The country has many castles and archaeological sites. Capital Tirana centers on sprawling Skanderbeg Square, site of the National History Museum, with exhibits spanning antiquity to post-communism, and frescoed Et’hem Bey Mosque.

items.title: Austria 2 Kronen [Uni Face]
items.description: Austria, formally the Republic of Austria, is a landlocked country in Central Europe, lying in the Eastern Alps. It is a federation of nine states, of which the capital Vienna is the most populous city and statev

items.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina 50 Dinara
items.description: Bosnia and Herzegovina is a country on the Balkan Peninsula in southeastern Europe. Its countryside is home to medieval villages, rivers and lakes, plus the craggy Dinaric Alps. National capital Sarajevo has a well preserved old quarter, Baščaršija, with landmarks like 16th-century Gazi Husrev-bey Mosque. Ottoman-era Latin Bridge is the site of the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand, which ignited World War I

items.title: Bulgaria 20 Leva : P-100
items.description: Bulgaria is a Balkan nation with diverse terrain encompassing Black Sea coastline, a mountainous interior and rivers, including the Danube. A cultural melting pot with Greek, Slavic, Ottoman, and Persian influences, it has a rich heritage of traditional dance, music, costumes, and crafts. At the foot of domed Vitosha mountain is its capital city, Sofia, dating to the 5th century B.C.

items.title: Croatia 1 Hrvatski Dinar : P-16
items.description: Croatia, officially the Republic of Croatia, is a country in Central and Southeast Europe, on the coast of the Adriatic Sea. It borders Slovenia to the northwest, Hungary to the northeast, Serbia to the east, Bosnia and Herzegovina and Montenegro to the southeast, and shares a maritime border with Italy to the west.

items.title: Cyprus 1 Pound : 60d(1)
items.description: Cyprus is the third-largest Mediterranean island, strategically located near Turkey, Syria, and Egypt. It is divided into the internationally recognized Republic of Cyprus (EU member) in the south and the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus in the north. Nicosia is the capital, while Paphos and Ayia Napa are major tourist spots.

items.title: Czechia 100 Korun P18
items.description: The Czech Republic, also known as Czechia and historically known as Bohemia, is a landlocked country in Central Europe. The country is bordered by Austria to the south, Germany to the west, Poland to the northeast, and Slovakia to the southeast.

items.title: Czechoslovakia 10Korun P94a
items.description: Czechoslovakia was a central European country formed in 1918 following World War I from the breakup of the Austro-Hungarian Empire. It was known as a prosperous industrial democracy before WWII and later fell under Soviet communist rule until the 1989 Velvet Revolution. The state peacefully dissolved on January 1, 1993, dividing into the Czech Republic and Slovakia

items.title: Denmark 50 Kroner P 65h(2)
items.description: Denmark is a Scandinavian country comprising the Jutland Peninsula and numerous islands. It's linked to nearby Sweden via the Öresund bridge. Copenhagen, its capital, is home to royal palaces and colorful Nyhavn harbor, plus the Tivoli amusement park and the iconic “Little Mermaid” statue. Odense is writer Hans Christian Andersen’s hometown, with a medieval core of cobbled streets and half-timbered houses.

items.title: Estonia  2 Krooni P85b
items.description: Estonia, a country in Northern Europe, borders the Baltic Sea and Gulf of Finland. Including more than 1,500 islands, its diverse terrain spans rocky beaches, old-growth forest and many lakes. Formerly part of the Soviet Union, it's dotted with castles, churches and hilltop fortresses. The capital, Tallinn, is known for its preserved Old Town, museums and the 314m-high Tallinn TV Tower, which has an observation deck

items.title: Finland 1 Markka 98a(30)
items.description: Finland is a Northern European nation bordering Sweden, Norway and Russia. Its capital, Helsinki, occupies a peninsula and surrounding islands in the Baltic Sea. Helsinki is home to the 18th-century sea fortress Suomenlinna, the fashionable Design District and diverse museums. The Northern Lights can be seen from the country's Arctic Lapland province, a vast wilderness with national parks and ski resorts.

items.title: France 20 Francs P-151d
items.description: France, in Western Europe, encompasses medieval cities, alpine villages and Mediterranean beaches. Paris, its capital, is famed for its fashion houses, classical art museums including the Louvre and monuments like the Eiffel Tower. The country is also renowned for its wines and sophisticated cuisine. Lascaux’s ancient cave drawings, Lyon’s Roman theater and the vast Palace of Versailles attest to its rich history.

items.title: Gibraltar 	10 Shillings P-W41
items.description: Gibraltar is a British Overseas Territory located at the southern tip of Spain, known for its strategic location at the Mediterranean entrance, the iconic 426m-high Rock of Gibraltar, and free-roaming Barbary macaques. It offers a unique mix of British culture—including red phone boxes and English language—with Mediterranean sunshine and Spanish influence.

items.title: England [Great Britain] 5 Pound P-394
items.description: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom. It is located on the island of Great Britain, of which it covers about 62%, and more than 100 smaller adjacent islands.

items.title: Greece 10 Drachmai P-314
items.description: Greece is a country in southeastern Europe with thousands of islands throughout the Aegean and Ionian seas. Influential in ancient times, it's often called the cradle of Western civilization. Athens, its capital, retains landmarks including the 5th-century B.C. Acropolis citadel with the Parthenon temple. Greece is also known for its beaches, from the black sands of Santorini to the party resorts of Mykonos

items.title: Greece 2 Drachmai P-318
items.description: Greece is a country in southeastern Europe with thousands of islands throughout the Aegean and Ionian seas. Influential in ancient times, it's often called the cradle of Western civilization. Athens, its capital, retains landmarks including the 5th-century B.C. Acropolis citadel with the Parthenon temple. Greece is also known for its beaches, from the black sands of Santorini to the party resorts of Mykonos

items.title: Hungary 500 Forint P-W202
items.description: Hungary is a landlocked country in Central Europe. Its capital, Budapest, is bisected by the Danube River. Its cityscape is studded with architectural landmarks from Buda’s medieval Castle Hill and grand neoclassical buildings along Pest’s Andrássy Avenue to the 19th-century Chain Bridge. Turkish and Roman influence on Hungarian culture includes the popularity of mineral spas, including at thermal Lake Hévíz.

items.title: Iceland 10 Krónur 48a(2)
items.description: Iceland, a Nordic island nation, is defined by its dramatic landscape with volcanoes, geysers, hot springs and lava fields. Massive glaciers are protected in Vatnajökull and Snæfellsjökull national parks. Most of the population lives in the capital, Reykjavik, which runs on geothermal power and is home to the National and Saga museums, tracing Iceland’s Viking history.

items.title: Italy 1000 Lire P-114b
items.description: Italy, a European country with a long Mediterranean coastline, has left a powerful mark on Western culture and cuisine. Its capital, Rome, is home to the Vatican as well as landmark art and ancient ruins. Other major cities include Florence, with Renaissance masterpieces such as Michelangelo’s "David" and Brunelleschi's Duomo; Venice, the city of canals; and Milan, Italy’s fashion capital

items.title: Lithuania 1 Talonas P-39
items.description: Lithuania is a democratic Baltic state in Northern Europe with a population of roughly 2.9 million. Its capital is Vilnius. A member of the EU, NATO, and the Eurozone, Lithuania features a high-income economy. Known for its forests, 6,000 lakes, and Baltic coastline, it has a deep history as a former 15th-century superpower and was the first Soviet republic to declare independence in 1990.

items.title: Latvia 1 Latvijas Rublis P-35
items.description: Latvia is a Baltic nation in Northern Europe, bordered by Estonia, Lithuania, Russia, Belarus, and the Baltic Sea. With a population of 1.83 million and its capital in Riga, this EU and NATO member is known for its temperate climate, extensive forests, and a mix of medieval and Art Nouveau architecture. The country offers a high quality of life, using the Euro and featuring a vibrant culture with strong roots in song and dance traditions.

items.title: Luxembourg 100 Francs P58b
items.description: Luxembourg is a small, landlocked parliamentary constitutional monarchy in Western Europe, bordered by Belgium, France, and Germany. It is known as one of the world's wealthiest nations, with the highest GDP per capita in the EU. The country is trilingual (French, German, Luxembourgish) and is a major, central hub for European institutions.

items.title: Moldova 1 Leu P-21
items.description: Moldova, an Eastern European country and former Soviet republic, has varied terrain including forests, rocky hills and vineyards. Its wine regions include Nistreana, known for reds, and Codru, home to some of the world’s largest cellars. Capital Chișinău has Soviet-style architecture and the National Museum of History, exhibiting art and ethnographic collections that reflect cultural links with neighboring Romania.

items.title: North Macedonia 50 Denari P-26
items.description: North Macedonia, officially the Republic of North Macedonia, is a landlocked country in Southeastern Europe. Formerly known as Macedonia, it changed its name in 2019 to resolve a long-standing dispute with Greece. It is a parliamentary republic known for its mountainous terrain, rich history, and the capital city, Skopje.

items.title: North Macedonia 10 Denari P-W27
items.description: North Macedonia, officially the Republic of North Macedonia, is a landlocked country in Southeastern Europe. Formerly known as Macedonia, it changed its name in 2019 to resolve a long-standing dispute with Greece. It is a parliamentary republic known for its mountainous terrain, rich history, and the capital city, Skopje.

items.title: Norway 200 Kroner
items.description: Norway is a Scandinavian country encompassing mountains, glaciers and deep coastal fjords. Oslo, the capital, is a city of green spaces and museums. Preserved 9th-century Viking ships are displayed at Oslo’s Viking Ship Museum. Bergen, with colorful wooden houses, is the starting point for cruises to the dramatic Sognefjord. Norway is also known for fishing, hiking and skiing, notably at Lillehammer’s Olympic resort.

items.title: Poland 100 Złotych P-143e
items.description: Poland, officially the Republic of Poland, is a country in Central Europe. It extends from the Baltic Sea in the north to the Sudetes and Carpathian Mountains in the south.

items.title: Poland 500 Złotych P-144d
items.description: Poland, officially the Republic of Poland, is a country in Central Europe. It extends from the Baltic Sea in the north to the Sudetes and Carpathian Mountains in the south.

items.title: Portugal 100 Escudos 179d(7)
items.description: Portugal, located on the Iberian Peninsula in Southwestern Europe, is a historic nation known for its 1,700 km coastline, warm climate, and vibrant culture. With a population of over 10.7 million, it is a stable, democratic republic with Lisbon as its capital and largest city. Portugal is highly regarded for its diverse landscapes, including the volcanic Azores and Madeira archipelagos.

items.title: Romania 2.000 Lei P-111b
items.description: Romania is a southeastern European country known for the forested region of Transylvania, ringed by the Carpathian Mountains. Its preserved medieval towns include Sighişoara, and there are many fortified churches and castles, notably clifftop Bran Castle, long associated with the Dracula legend. Bucharest, the country’s capital, is the site of the gigantic, Communist-era Palatul Parlamentului government building.

items.title: Romania 1 Leu P-117
items.description: Romania is a southeastern European country known for the forested region of Transylvania, ringed by the Carpathian Mountains. Its preserved medieval towns include Sighişoara, and there are many fortified churches and castles, notably clifftop Bran Castle, long associated with the Dracula legend. Bucharest, the country’s capital, is the site of the gigantic, Communist-era Palatul Parlamentului government building.

items.title: Tatarstan 100 Rubley P-5a/b/c
items.description: Tatarstan, officially the Republic of Tatarstan, sometimes also called Tataria, is a republic of Russia located in Eastern Europe. It is a part of the Volga Federal District; and its capital and largest city is Kazan, an important cultural centre in Russia.

items.title: Serbia 10 Dinara P-46
items.description: Serbia, officially the Republic of Serbia, is a landlocked country in Southeast and Central Europe

items.title: Serbia 20 Dinara P-47
items.description: Serbia, officially the Republic of Serbia, is a landlocked country in Southeast and Central Europe

items.title: Slovakia 20 Slovenských Korún P-20g
items.description: Slovakia, officially the Slovak Republic, is a landlocked country in Central Europe with a population of approximately 5.4 million. Established in 1993 after the dissolution of Czechoslovakia, it is a parliamentary republic with a high-income economy. Bratislava is the capital city. The country is characterized by the Tatra Mountains, the Danube River, and a strong automotive manufacturing sector.

items.title: Slovenia 50 Tolarjev P-13a
items.description: Slovenia, a country in Central Europe, is known for its mountains, ski resorts and lakes. On Lake Bled, a glacial lake fed by hot springs, the town of Bled contains a church-topped islet and a cliffside medieval castle. In Ljubljana, Slovenia’s capital, baroque facades mix with the 20th-century architecture of native Jože Plečnik, whose iconic Tromostovje (Triple Bridge) spans the tightly curving Ljubljanica River.

items.title: Spain 1000 Pesetas P-158
items.description: Spain, a country on Europe’s Iberian Peninsula, includes 17 autonomous regions with diverse geography and cultures. Capital city Madrid is home to the Royal Palace and Prado museum, housing works by European masters. Segovia has a medieval castle (the Alcázar) and an intact Roman aqueduct. Catalonia’s capital, Barcelona, is defined by Antoni Gaudí’s whimsical modernist landmarks like the Sagrada Família church.

items.title: Sweden 5 Kronor P-51d
items.description: Sweden is a Scandinavian nation with thousands of coastal islands and inland lakes, along with vast boreal forests and glaciated mountains. Its principal cities, eastern capital Stockholm and southwestern Gothenburg and Malmö, are all coastal. Stockholm is built on 14 islands. It has more than 50 bridges, as well as the medieval old town, Gamla Stan, royal palaces and museums such as open-air Skansen.

items.title: Ukraine 1 Hrivnya P-116Ac
items.description: Ukraine is a country in Eastern Europe. It is the second-largest country in Europe after Russia, which borders it to the east and northeast.

items.title: Ukraine 5 Hriven' P-118e
items.description: Ukraine is a country in Eastern Europe. It is the second-largest country in Europe after Russia, which borders it to the east and northeast.

items.title: EU 5 Euro P-1s(2)
items.description: The European Union (EU) is a unique political and economic union of 27 European member states covering over 4 million Km2 and hosting roughly 451 million people (2025). It operates as a single market, with the euro as its currency in most states, fostering stability, economic growth, and the free movement of people, goods, services, and capital.
